title: Securing Claims Through a Bailiff – Interim Measures in Polish Law
description: Interim security measures protect creditors from asset dissipation before a judgment is obtained. Learn how a court bailiff executes security orders in Poland.
content:
What Is Securing a Claim?

Securing a claim (zabezpieczenie roszczenia) is a provisional legal measure designed to protect a creditor against a debtor concealing or transferring assets before a final judgment is obtained. Unlike formal enforcement proceedings, securing a claim does not satisfy the debt – it merely preserves the possibility of future enforcement.

Legal Basis

Security measures in Poland are governed by Arts. 730–757 of the Code of Civil Procedure. A security order is issued by the court on the application of the entitled party (creditor) and then forwarded to the bailiff for execution.

When Should You Apply for Security?

An application for security is justified when:

  • the debtor shows signs of insolvency (disposing of assets, closing bank accounts),
  • the claim is credible (supported by documents, invoices, contracts),
  • there is a risk that without security, enforcement of a future judgment will be impossible or significantly hampered.

Types of Security Measures

The court may grant security by:

  • Seizure of movable assets, wages or bank accounts – temporary freezing of the debtor's funds or assets,
  • Compulsory mortgage on real property – a land register entry ensuring priority in satisfaction,
  • Prohibition on disposing of or encumbering real property – preventing the debtor from selling or mortgaging real estate,
  • Other appropriate measures suited to the circumstances of the case.

How Does the Bailiff Execute a Security Order?

Upon receiving the security order, the bailiff acts promptly: seizing receivables (bank accounts, wages, debts owed to the debtor), drawing up inventory protocols for movable assets, or applying for registration of a compulsory mortgage. The Bailiff's Office No. IX in Otwock uses modern IT systems ensuring swift execution of security orders.

Duration of Security

Security measures expire one month after the judgment granting the secured claim becomes final. The creditor should file an enforcement application within that period to continue recovery through formal enforcement proceedings.

Costs

The fee for executing a security measure is 5% of the value of the secured claim. The creditor initially bears the cost, which is later recovered from the debtor during subsequent enforcement proceedings.

metaDescription: Interim security measures executed by a court bailiff in Poland. When to apply, types of measures and costs. Bailiff's Office No. IX, Marcin Starzec, Otwock.
